bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am targeting my maximum utility bid. Since there is no time pressure at t=0.0, I should start firm on my preferred values across all issues and wait for the opponent’s response.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (~0.98) because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s only offer is the minimum value on every issue for me, and their message about authorization is cheap talk not supported by any concession history. I will make a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the most important issues D and E.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.04 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer and their authorization claims are cheap talk, so I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the high-weight issues C, D, and E.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making modest concessions on my lowest-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain extremely poor for me, with only a small move on issueE, and their authorization claims are cheap talk contradicted by their actual concession. I will hold firm on high-weight issueD and issueE while conceding issueA and issueB to valueD.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.94, since it is still very early and the opponent’s offers remain far below acceptable value despite a small concession on issueE. Their repeated “authorization” claims are cheap talk; the actual bids show only minimal movement, so I am conceding only on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueC, issueD, and issueE.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on a low-weight issue to keep movement visible. The opponent’s messages claim they cannot improve, but their actual offers have changed slightly on issueE and issueD, so I treat the claims as cheap talk and continue holding firm on my high-weight issues C/D/E.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.90 because it is still early in the negotiation, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent’s messages claiming authorization limits are not credible by themselves, and their actual bids show only small concessions, mostly on issueE/issueD, which are high-importance issues for me. I will continue conceding mainly on low-weight issueA and issueB while holding issueC, issueD, and issueE at my best values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 because it is still early in the negotiation, so there is no need to concede on my high-weight issues yet. The opponent’s offers and messages show mostly firm positioning with only small movement on issueD and issueE, but their “authorization” claims are cheap talk; I will concede first on low-weight issueA while holding issueC, issueD, and issueE at my best values.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.86 because it is still early at t=0.16, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me, from about 0.20 to about 0.32, despite repeated “final authority” messages, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and rely on the bid pattern. I am conceding mainly on low-weight issues A and B while holding firm on the more important issues C, D, and E.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.18, so there is no need to make major concessions on the high-weight issues D and E. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim they cannot move, but their actual offers have changed, including a major concession to issueE=valueE, so I trust the bid history over the cheap talk. I will concede further on the low-weight issueB while holding issueC, issueD, and issueE at my preferred values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 because it is still early at t=0.20, so there is no need to move close to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim they cannot move, but their bids have in fact changed, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and rely on the offer pattern. I have already conceded on low-weight issues A and B, and now make a moderate concession on issueC while holding firm on the high-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 because the negotiation is only at t=0.22, so there is no need to move close to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s offers have improved slightly for me, with their best recent bids worth about 0.44, but their repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their actual concessions. I have already conceded the low-weight issues A and B, so I am making a measured concession on issueC while holding firm on the high-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 because it is early-mid negotiation time (t=0.24), so I should concede gradually but keep firm on the high-weight issuesD and issueE. The opponent’s repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk; their actual offers show only small movement, mainly on issueB/issueD/issueE, so I am making a modest concession on lower/mid-weight issues while preserving my key value on issueD and issueE.

At t=0.26 I am still targeting a solid utility around 0.72 rather than accepting the opponent’s much lower offers; their best offers to me have only reached about 0.44 and their latest is lower. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their changing bids, so I rely on the offer pattern: I concede mainly on low-weight issues A and B and keep firm on high-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting about 0.74 utility, still protecting my highest-weight issues issueD and issueE while making concessions on lower-weight issueA/issueB and some movement on issueC. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not concede heavily on the most important issues yet; the opponent’s repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their actual gradual movement, especially on issueE and occasionally issueD/issueB.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.66 by conceding further on the lower-to-medium weight issues while holding firm on issueD and issueE, which are most important to me. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly and their repeated “authorization” claims are cheap talk, but their bids suggest they are pushing hard for low values across most issues. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure to move, but not enough to sacrifice my highest-weight issues yet.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.62 while making a meaningful concession from my prior position because we are about one-third through the negotiation and the opponent’s offers have only modestly improved for me. The opponent’s repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk, but their actual bids show some willingness to move on issueD and issueE, so I am preserving my highest-weight issueE while conceding issueD one step and leaving lower-value issues at their most concessive levels.

I am targeting about 0.62 utility, maintaining value on the two most important issues while keeping the lower-weight issues at concessions already shown. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I will not drop to the opponent’s best offer yet; their offers have improved somewhat despite repeated “no further authority” messages, so I treat those claims as cheap talk and focus on their revealed willingness to move on issueD/issueE.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64, which is a moderate concession from my ideal but still protects my highest-weight issues, especially issueE and issueD. At t=0.36 there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s best offer yet, but their latest bid shows movement toward valueE on issueE and valueB on issueB, while their repeated “authorization” claims are contradicted by continued changes. I am matching some of their signaled positions on lower-weight issues and asking for valueD on issueD as the main remaining tradeoff.

At t=0.38 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.62, continuing concessions on the low-weight issues while protecting the high-weight issueD/issueE bundle. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim no authority to move, but their actual offers have changed over time, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and focus on the bids; their best offers to me have improved only into the low-to-mid utility range, so I should not concede too sharply yet.
